body.text: In the military, I got married. I got pregnant. And at four years of service — with everything still ahead of me in uniform — I chose a different path.
For years, I carried that choice like a wound. The shame of leaving early. The grief of an identity I'd poured myself into. The question that wouldn't quiet: What would I have become?

But honest reckoning rewrites the story. I didn't abandon my service — I completed it on my own terms. Every crew I led, every mission I owned, every standard I upheld came with me. What I carried out of that chapter is worth more than what I left behind.
